# WebWaka OS — Verticals Execution Prompts: Financial + Place + Media + Institutional

**Document type:** Agent execution prompt set
**Scope:** Financial (4) + Place (4) + Media (3) + Institutional (2) extended verticals — 13 verticals covered
**Repo:** https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os
**Branch base:** `main`
**Milestone targets:** M9–M12
**Status:** Ready for agent execution — P1 Financial verticals complete (M8e)
**Generated by:** MASTER_CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md — Set I

---

> **3-in-1 Platform Note:**
> Financial verticals primarily serve **Pillar 1 (Ops)** and **Pillar 3 (Marketplace)**.
> Place verticals serve all three pillars — Pillar 2 for branded venue sites.
> Media verticals serve **Pillar 1 (Ops)**, **Pillar 2 (Branding)**, and **Pillar 3 (Marketplace)**.
> Institutional verticals serve **Pillar 1 (Ops)** and **Pillar 3** only.
> **SuperAgent AI is cross-cutting — NOT a fourth pillar.**
> **Critical AI governance for this set:**
> - Financial verticals: **L2 maximum for cash flow forecasts — NO automated financial transaction suggestions** (CBN consumer protection); BDC and mobile money: Tier 3 KYC mandatory
> - Media (NBC-regulated content): **L3 HITL for broadcast content scheduling**
> - Government agency vertical: **L3 HITL for procurement or budget AI output**
> - All transactions in integer kobo (P9); no floats anywhere in this set

### General rules for all agents using these prompts

- Never make assumptions about WebWaka's architecture. Always read the referenced documents and code first.
- All financial transaction amounts in integer kobo (P9) — this is a hard rule for all verticals in this set.
- FX-related amounts (BDC): store naira equivalent in kobo + USD amount in integer cents — no floats.
- No cross-tenant data leakage (T3). Every D1 query scopes to `tenant_id`.
- CBN regulatory data must not be stored beyond consent scope (NDPR P10).

---

## FINANCIAL VERTICALS

---

## TASK V-FIN-EXT-1: Airtime / VTU Reseller Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `airtime-reseller`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Airtime/VTU (Virtual Top-Up) reselling is a major micro-business in Nigeria; over 2 million active resellers; NCC and CBN agent banking compliance apply
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:**
  - Verticals master plan: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md
  - 3-in-1 platform architecture: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md
  - Verticals FSM engine: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/packages/verticals/src/
  - Platform invariants: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/platform-invariants.md
  - Entitlement model: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/entitlement-model.md
  - AI integration framework: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md
  - SuperAgent product spec: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/superagent/02-product-spec.md

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in telecom retail and micro-finance services, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian VTU/airtime reseller sector — NCC (Nigerian Communications Commission) dealer registration, MTN/Airtel/Glo/9mobile dealer framework, CBN agent banking sub-agent rules, daily transaction caps (₦300,000/day for sub-agents)
- Stock balance management (airtime wallet balance in kobo), commission tracking (commission_bps INTEGER basis points), transaction log, NCC dealer code
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — Financial verticals; entry for `airtime-reseller`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `airtime-reseller`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine; `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`; `infra/db/migrations/` — next migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian airtime reseller market — NCC licensed dealers, VTU aggregator platforms (VTpass, Gladtidings), daily transaction volume, average reseller margin (2–5%); competitors: VTpass, Gladtidings, Bills.ng; key features: stock balance (wallet_balance_kobo), transaction log (network: MTN/Airtel/Glo/9mobile, recipient_phone, amount_kobo, commission_kobo, transaction_date), daily revenue summary, NCC dealer code tracking, CBN sub-agent daily limit enforcement (₦300,000 = 30,000,000 kobo cap).

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian airtime/VTU resellers a digital transaction log, wallet balance tracker, commission record, and daily CBN cap enforcement — all in kobo
- **Schema:**
  - `airtime_reseller_profiles` — business_name, NCC_dealer_code, CBN_sub_agent_number (if applicable), workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `airtime_wallet` — reseller_id, wallet_balance_kobo, daily_used_kobo, daily_reset_date INTEGER, tenant_id
  - `airtime_transactions` — recipient_phone (for commission tracking — not PII export), network, amount_kobo, commission_kobo, transaction_date INTEGER, status (pending/completed/failed), tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `ncc_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `CASH_FLOW_FORECAST` (daily revenue trend) — L2; Growth plan; aggregate transaction counts and commission totals — no recipient phone (P13); NDPR; USSD excluded (P12)
- **Regulatory hook:** NCC dealer code at `ncc_verified`; CBN sub-agent number if applicable
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for standard reseller; Tier 3 if acting as CBN sub-agent
- **CRITICAL:** Daily transaction total must never exceed 30,000,000 kobo (₦300,000) for sub-agents — enforce at route level

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-airtime-reseller`. `registerAirtimeResellerVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. All amounts in kobo (P9). Commission as integer kobo (not percentage — calculated before storage). CBN daily cap enforced at API route level. Tenant scoped (T3). USSD excluded from AI (P12).

---

**4. QA:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-airtime-reseller/src/airtime-reseller.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `ncc_verified`; transaction with integer amount_kobo, commission_kobo; wallet balance deducted correctly; daily used sum correct; AI revenue trend with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: transaction that would exceed CBN daily cap (30M kobo) → blocked with typed error; fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI with recipient phone → P13 blocked; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; Tier 3 KYC for sub-agent → 403 without.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/airtime-reseller): Airtime/VTU Reseller vertical — transactions, CBN daily cap, NCC compliance (M12)"
git push origin feat/vertical-airtime-reseller
```
PR checklist: CBN daily cap enforcement test, NCC gate, `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`, labeled, KYC guards, SuperAgent L2, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

---

## TASK V-FIN-EXT-2: Bureau de Change / FX Dealer Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `bureau-de-change`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Nigeria has hundreds of CBN-licensed BDC operators; forex market is heavily regulated since 2021–2023 reforms; FX rate in kobo per USD cent (integer)
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in foreign exchange and financial services compliance, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian BDC sector — CBN BDC licence categories (Tier 1: minimum ₦2B capital; Tier 2: minimum ₦500M), CBN EFEMS (Electronic Foreign Exchange Management System) integration readiness, AML/CFT compliance, daily FX transaction cap, EFCC reporting threshold
- FX rates stored as integer kobo per USD cent (to avoid floats), transaction amounts in kobo, USD amounts in integer cents
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents. Note: FX rates must be stored as integer kobo per USD cent — NEVER as float exchange rates.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian BDC market post-2023 CBN reforms — ABCON (Association of Bureau De Change Operators of Nigeria), new tier structure, I&E FX window, BDC daily sales cap per customer (₦5,000 equivalent), AML/EFCC reporting above $10,000 equivalent; key features: rate management (buy_rate_kobo_per_usd_cent, sell_rate_kobo_per_usd_cent as integers), transaction log (customer_bvn_ref — hashed, currency, usd_amount_cents INTEGER, naira_amount_kobo, direction: buy/sell, transaction_date), CBN licence tracking, daily FX position, EFCC report flag.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `bdc_profiles` (company_name, CBN_BDC_licence, ABCON_membership, CBN_tier (1/2), CAC_RC, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `bdc_rates` (rate_date INTEGER, buy_rate_kobo_per_cent INTEGER, sell_rate_kobo_per_cent INTEGER, currency (USD/EUR/GBP/CNY), tenant_id); `bdc_transactions` (customer_bvn_ref TEXT (hashed), currency, usd_amount_cents INTEGER, naira_amount_kobo, direction, transaction_date INTEGER, efcc_report_required BOOLEAN, status (completed/reversed), tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cbn_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `CASH_FLOW_FORECAST` (FX position trend) — L2 maximum; NO automated FX rate suggestion (CBN regulated); aggregate transaction volume by direction — no customer BVN ref (P13); NDPR; USSD excluded (P12)
- **CRITICAL:** FX rates as integer kobo per USD cent (1 USD = 100 cents; rate e.g. 160,500 kobo/cent = ₦1,605/USD). No float rates stored anywhere.
- **KYC:** Tier 3 mandatory — FX dealing is regulated financial activity; BVN + NIN + EFCC compliance

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-bureau-de-change`. `registerBureauDeChangeVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. FX rates as integer kobo per USD cent (no floats). All naira amounts in kobo (P9). USD amounts in integer cents. Customer BVN as hashed reference (P13). Tier 3 KYC mandatory. Tenant scoped (T3). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `cbn_verified` (Tier 3 KYC required); rate with integer buy_rate_kobo_per_cent, sell_rate_kobo_per_cent; transaction with integer usd_amount_cents, naira_amount_kobo; EFCC flag set when threshold exceeded; AI position forecast with aiRights + NDPR (aggregate volume only); float rate → rejected; customer BVN in AI → P13 blocked; automated rate suggestion → L2 cap blocked; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Tier 3 KYC → 403 for lower tiers. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/bureau-de-change): BDC vertical — FX rates (integer kobo/cent), CBN compliance, EFCC, Tier 3 (M12)"`. PR checklist: integer FX rates (no floats), Tier 3 mandatory, CBN gate, EFCC flag, standard checks.

## TASK V-FIN-EXT-3: Hire Purchase / Asset Finance Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `hire-purchase`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Hire purchase (HP) and consumer asset finance for electronics, motorcycles, tricycles (Keke), and agricultural equipment is growing; CBN consumer credit rules, SEC oversight for securitised products
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in consumer credit and asset finance management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian HP/consumer credit sector — CBN consumer protection guidelines (2022 revision), SEC securitisation framework, credit bureau (CRC, First Central) enquiry readiness, repayment schedule in kobo (integer installment amounts), asset tracking (motorcycle VIN, electronics serial number), default management.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Key features: asset register (asset_type: motorcycle/electronics/agricultural_equipment, serial_number TEXT, asset_value_kobo), HP agreement (customer_bvn_ref hashed, asset_id, total_hp_value_kobo, deposit_kobo, installments INTEGER, installment_amount_kobo, tenor_months INTEGER, start_date, status), repayment log (agreement_id, payment_date, amount_kobo, outstanding_kobo), default log, CBN consumer protection flag.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `hire_purchase_profiles` (company_name, CBN_consumer_credit_reg, CAC_RC, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `hp_assets` (asset_type, serial_number TEXT, asset_value_kobo, status (available/on_hp/repossessed), tenant_id); `hp_agreements` (customer_bvn_ref TEXT, asset_id, total_hp_value_kobo, deposit_kobo, installments INTEGER, installment_amount_kobo, tenor_months INTEGER, start_date INTEGER, outstanding_kobo, status (active/completed/defaulted/repossessed), tenant_id); `hp_repayments` (agreement_id, payment_date INTEGER, amount_kobo, outstanding_after_kobo, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cbn_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `CASH_FLOW_FORECAST` (repayment collection forecast) — L2; Pro plan; aggregate repayment rates by asset type — no customer BVN ref (P13); NDPR; USSD excluded (P12)
- **KYC:** Tier 3 mandatory — consumer credit operations; BVN + NIN + income verification

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-hire-purchase`. `registerHirePurchaseVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. All monetary in kobo (P9). Installments and tenor as integers. Customer BVN hashed. Tier 3 required. Tenant scoped (T3). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `cbn_verified`; asset with integer asset_value_kobo; agreement with integer total_hp_value_kobo, deposit_kobo, installment_amount_kobo, tenor_months; repayment logs; outstanding_kobo decrements correctly; AI repayment forecast with aiRights + NDPR; BVN in AI → P13 blocked; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error; Tier 3 KYC enforced. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/hire-purchase): Hire Purchase vertical — HP agreements, repayment schedule, CBN compliance (M12)"`. PR checklist: Tier 3, kobo arithmetic (outstanding decrement), P13 BVN exclusion, standard checks.

## TASK V-FIN-EXT-4: Mobile Money Agent Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `mobile-money-agent`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — CBN's mobile money super-agent/sub-agent framework has empowered thousands of agents (OPay, Moniepoint, PalmPay, Kuda agent banking); daily transaction caps strictly enforced
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in mobile money and agent banking management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian mobile money sector — CBN agent banking framework (super-agent licence, sub-agent network), OPay/Moniepoint/PalmPay/FirstMonie agent types, daily transaction caps (sub-agent: ₦300,000/day; super-agent: ₦1,000,000/day), float management (float_balance_kobo), transaction types (cash-in, cash-out, transfer, bill payment, airtime).

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Key features: agent register (CBN_sub_agent_number, super_agent_name: OPay/Moniepoint/etc, float_balance_kobo), transaction log (transaction_type: cash_in/cash_out/transfer/bill/airtime, amount_kobo, commission_kobo, customer_bvn_ref hashed, transaction_date, reference_number TEXT), daily position (daily_used_kobo, daily_limit_kobo), float top-up log.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `mobile_money_agent_profiles` (agent_name, CBN_sub_agent_number, super_agent_provider, super_agent_licence_number, CAC_or_tin, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `mm_float` (agent_id, float_balance_kobo, daily_used_kobo, daily_limit_kobo, last_topup_kobo, last_topup_date INTEGER, tenant_id); `mm_transactions` (transaction_type, amount_kobo, commission_kobo, customer_bvn_ref TEXT, reference_number TEXT, transaction_date INTEGER, status (completed/failed/reversed), tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cbn_agent_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `CASH_FLOW_FORECAST` (daily float utilisation trend) — L2; Growth plan; aggregate transaction counts and volume by type — no customer BVN ref (P13); NDPR; USSD excluded from AI (P12) — note: regular transactions CAN use USSD (the business channel); only AI advisory blocked on USSD
- **CRITICAL:** Daily transaction total must not exceed daily_limit_kobo; enforced at route level; default sub-agent limit = 30,000,000 kobo (₦300,000)
- **KYC:** Tier 3 mandatory — agent banking involves regulated financial intermediation

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-mobile-money-agent`. `registerMobileMoneyAgentVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. All amounts in kobo (P9). Customer BVN hashed. Daily cap enforced at route level. Tier 3 required. Tenant scoped (T3). Only AI advisory blocked on USSD — standard transaction routes can function on USSD.

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `cbn_agent_verified`; transaction with integer amount_kobo, commission_kobo; float balance decrements correctly; daily_used_kobo accumulates; transaction exceeding daily cap → blocked with typed error; float top-up increases balance; AI forecast with aiRights + NDPR; customer BVN in AI → P13 blocked; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error; Tier 3 KYC enforced. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/mobile-money-agent): Mobile Money Agent vertical — float mgmt, CBN daily cap, Tier 3 (M12)"`. PR checklist: daily cap enforcement, Tier 3, P13 BVN exclusion, float arithmetic, standard checks.

## TASK V-PLC-EXT-1: Event Hall / Venue Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `event-hall`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — Event halls are booked continuously in Nigerian cities for weddings, corporate events, church programmes; Lagos, Abuja, Port Harcourt; state event licence and fire safety certificate required
- **Milestone target:** M10
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 2 (Branding) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in event venue management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian event hall sector — state event licence (LSPC in Lagos, FCT Events Commission in Abuja), fire safety certificate (state Fire Service), capacity management, booking pipeline, deposit management in kobo; popular venue sizes (500–5,000 guests); catering and AV add-ons; LAWMA waste management compliance.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian event hall market — Lagos event hall density, average hall hire rate (₦200,000–₦5M per day), wedding vs corporate booking ratio; competitors: Eventbrite for discovery, local WhatsApp management; key features: hall profile (capacity_guests INTEGER, amenities: AC/projector/catering_kitchen, daily_hire_rate_kobo), booking management (event_date, event_type, client_phone, hire_rate_kobo, deposit_kobo, balance_kobo, status), add-on services (catering/AV/security), blocked dates calendar, state event licence tracking.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `event_hall_profiles` (hall_name, state_event_licence, fire_safety_cert, CAC_RC, capacity_guests INTEGER, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `hall_bookings` (client_phone, event_date INTEGER, event_type, capacity_required INTEGER, hire_rate_kobo, deposit_kobo, balance_kobo, add_ons JSON, status (enquiry/confirmed/completed/cancelled), tenant_id); `hall_blocked_dates` (blocked_date INTEGER, reason, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `licence_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `VENUE_UTILIZATION_REPORT` (booking rate by event type, seasonal peaks) — L2; Growth plan; aggregate counts and revenue by event type — no client details (P13); NDPR; USSD excluded (P12)
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for booking deposits; Tier 3 for corporate contracts above ₦5M

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-event-hall`. `registerEventHallVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. Capacity as integer. Kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `licence_verified`; booking with integer hire_rate_kobo, deposit_kobo; capacity as integer; blocked date set; double-booking same date rejected (conflict check test); AI utilisation with aiRights + NDPR; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; KYC 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/event-hall): Event Hall vertical — bookings, double-booking prevention, AI utilisation (M10)"`. PR checklist: double-booking conflict test, state licence gate, standard checks.

## TASK V-PLC-EXT-2: Water Treatment / Borehole Operator Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `water-treatment`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — Distinct from Set C `water-vendor`; this is the formal water treatment plant and borehole utility operator serving communities, estates, and commercial clients — NAFDAC and state water board full compliance required
- **Milestone target:** M11
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in water utility operations management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian water treatment sector — NAFDAC water treatment licence, state water board operator certification, water quality testing standards (WHO/SON), estate water distribution billing; water volume in integer litres; chlorine levels in integer parts-per-million × 10 (to avoid floats); monthly subscription billing in kobo.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Key features: plant register (capacity_litres_per_day INTEGER, NAFDAC_water_licence, quality_cert), water quality log (test_date, ph_level INTEGER × 100, chlorine_ppm10 INTEGER, turbidity_ntu10 INTEGER — all stored as scaled integers), client subscription (client_phone, property_type: residential/commercial/estate, monthly_rate_kobo, daily_litres_allocation INTEGER), billing log, maintenance record.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `water_treatment_profiles` (company_name, NAFDAC_water_licence, state_water_board_cert, CAC_RC, capacity_litres_per_day INTEGER, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `water_quality_log` (test_date INTEGER, ph_x100 INTEGER, chlorine_ppm10 INTEGER, turbidity_ntu10 INTEGER, passed_standards BOOLEAN, tenant_id); `water_subscriptions` (client_phone, property_type, monthly_rate_kobo, daily_litres_allocation INTEGER, payment_status, tenant_id); `water_billing` (client_phone, billing_month TEXT, volume_supplied_litres INTEGER, billed_kobo, paid_kobo, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `nafdac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `COLD_CHAIN_ANOMALY` adapted as `WATER_QUALITY_ALERT` (quality trend analysis) — L2; Growth plan; aggregate quality stats — no client details (P13); NDPR; USSD excluded (P12)
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for subscription billing; Tier 3 for estate utility contracts above ₦5M/month

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-water-treatment`. `registerWaterTreatmentVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. All measures as scaled integers (pH × 100, chlorine × 10, turbidity × 10). Volume in integer litres. Kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `nafdac_verified`; quality log with integer ph_x100 (e.g., 7.2 → 720), chlorine_ppm10; subscription with integer monthly_rate_kobo; billing with integer volume_supplied_litres; quality fail flag when below standard; AI alert with aiRights + NDPR; float pH rejected; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; KYC 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/water-treatment): Water Treatment vertical — quality log, subscriptions, NAFDAC, AI alerts (M11)"`. PR checklist: scaled integer tests (pH × 100), float rejection, NAFDAC gate, standard checks.

## TASK V-PLC-EXT-3: Community Hall / Town Hall Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `community-hall`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Community halls (CDCs, town union halls, clan meeting halls) are civic infrastructure across Nigerian cities and villages; LGA oversight, community development association management
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in community infrastructure management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian community hall sector — CDA (Community Development Associations), town union halls, LGA management; booking management, maintenance fund, hall dues collection in kobo; Tier 1 KYC for informal community use.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Key features: hall profile (capacity, LGA, CDA_registration), booking (group_name, event_type, booking_date, hire_fee_kobo, deposit_kobo, status), maintenance fund log (contribution_kobo, contributor_ref, date), hall rules documentation reference.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `community_hall_profiles` (hall_name, CDA_registration, LGA, state, capacity_seats INTEGER, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `community_hall_bookings` (group_name, event_type, booking_date INTEGER, hire_fee_kobo, deposit_kobo, status (booked/confirmed/completed/cancelled), tenant_id); `community_hall_maintenance` (contribution_date INTEGER, contributor_ref TEXT, amount_kobo, purpose, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `active` (3-state — community halls are informal civic infrastructure)
- **SuperAgent:** `VENUE_UTILIZATION_REPORT` (booking frequency by event type) — L1; Growth plan; aggregate only (P13); NDPR; USSD excluded (P12)
- **KYC:** Tier 1 sufficient

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in advertising agency management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian advertising sector — APCON (Advertising Practitioners Council of Nigeria) registration, OAAN (Outdoor Advertising Association of Nigeria) for OOH media, NAN (Nigerian Ad Network), NIPR overlap for PR; campaign management, media buying, client retainer billing in kobo
- P13: client campaign strategy and creative briefs are confidential — only billing aggregates to AI
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian advertising market — APCON registered agencies count, NAN digital advertising volume, OAAN outdoor spend, average agency commission (15% of media buy); key features: client account (client_ref_id opaque), campaign register (campaign_name, campaign_type: digital/OOH/TV/radio/print, budget_kobo, start_date, end_date, status), media buy log (channel, spend_kobo, impressions INTEGER), agency commission (commission_kobo), retainer billing, APCON licence tracking per account manager.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `advertising_agency_profiles` (agency_name, APCON_registration, OAAN_membership, CAC_RC, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `ad_campaigns` (client_ref_id TEXT, campaign_name, campaign_type, budget_kobo, start_date INTEGER, end_date INTEGER, status (planning/active/completed), tenant_id); `ad_media_buys` (campaign_id, channel, spend_kobo, impressions INTEGER, cpm_kobo INTEGER, tenant_id); `ad_billing` (client_ref_id, period TEXT, media_spend_kobo, agency_commission_kobo, retainer_kobo, total_billed_kobo, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `apcon_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `AD_CAMPAIGN_PERFORMANCE` (aggregate impressions and cost-per-mille trend), `BILLING_FORECAST` — L2; Growth plan; aggregate campaign metrics — no client_ref_id or creative brief (P13); NDPR; USSD excluded (P12)
- **Regulatory hook:** APCON registration number at `apcon_verified`; OAAN membership if OOH campaigns
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for campaign billing; Tier 3 for media buys above ₦50M (national media level)

## TASK V-INST-EXT-1: Government Agency / MDA Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `government-agency`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Nigerian federal and state MDAs (Ministries, Departments, and Agencies) need digital operations management; BPP procurement compliance, IPPIS payroll readiness, OAGF appropriation tracking; all amounts in kobo with strict integer enforcement
- **Milestone target:** M11
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in government digital transformation and public sector management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian public sector management — OAGF (Office of the Accountant-General of the Federation), IPPIS (Integrated Payroll and Personnel Information System), BPP (Bureau of Public Procurement) procurement threshold rules, TSA (Treasury Single Account) compliance
- Procurement tracking (tender amounts in kobo, BPP approval thresholds), appropriation line item management, staff payroll reference, agency revenue (IGR) collection
- **CRITICAL AI governance:** All AI output about government budget utilisation, procurement, or IGR must use L3 HITL — these are politically sensitive and auditor-sensitive outputs
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents + ADL political AI cap + L3 HITL rule.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian MDA digitisation — FIRS, NAFDAC, CBN, NDDC as examples of well-run MDAs; BPP procurement thresholds (N-Power, ERGP, SEAP); IPPIS payroll integration; TSA compliance; key features: appropriation register (budget_line_item, allocated_kobo, released_kobo, spent_kobo, fiscal_year), procurement log (procurement_ref TEXT, BPP_approval_ref, amount_kobo, vendor_ref TEXT, status), IGR collection log (revenue_type, collection_date, amount_kobo), staff register reference (IPPIS integration readiness — no raw staff details to AI).

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `government_agency_profiles` (agency_name, MDA_code TEXT, ministry_classification, BPP_registered, TSA_compliant, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `mda_appropriations` (fiscal_year TEXT, budget_line_item, allocated_kobo, released_kobo, spent_kobo, tenant_id); `mda_procurements` (procurement_ref TEXT, bpp_approval_ref TEXT, vendor_ref TEXT, amount_kobo, category (goods/services/works), status (open/evaluated/awarded/completed), tenant_id); `mda_igr_collections` (revenue_type, collection_date INTEGER, amount_kobo, receipt_ref TEXT, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `bpp_registered` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `CASH_FLOW_FORECAST` (appropriation utilisation rate) — **L3 HITL MANDATORY** for all government budget and procurement AI output; aggregate line item utilisation rates only — no vendor refs or procurement details (P13 + procurement privilege); NDPR; USSD excluded (P12)
- **Regulatory hook:** BPP registration + MDA code at `bpp_registered`; TSA compliance flag stored
- **KYC:** Tier 3 mandatory — government fund management requires full identity verification

---

**3. Implementation:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-government-agency`. `registerGovernmentAgencyVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. **L3 HITL mandatory on all AI.** All amounts in kobo (P9). Vendor_ref opaque. No procurement details in AI (P13). Tier 3 required. Tenant scoped (T3). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-government-agency/src/government-agency.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `bpp_registered`; appropriation line with integer allocated_kobo, released_kobo, spent_kobo; procurement with integer amount_kobo; IGR collection with integer amount_kobo; AI utilisation report with HITL + aiRights + NDPR (aggregate utilisation rates only). Negative: HITL flag missing → blocked; vendor details in AI → P13 blocked; fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error; Tier 3 KYC → 403 for lower tiers.

**CRITICAL TEST:** L3 HITL flag is required on ALL government agency AI calls — no exceptions.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/government-agency): Government Agency MDA vertical — appropriations, procurement, IGR, L3 HITL AI (M11)"
git push origin feat/vertical-government-agency
```
PR checklist: L3 HITL mandatory (all AI), Tier 3 KYC, BPP gate, P13 procurement data exclusion, standard checks.

## TASK V-INST-EXT-2: Polling Unit / Electoral District Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `polling-unit`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Nigeria's 176,000+ polling units are managed by INEC; ward-level electoral data management, voter count tracking, candidate result aggregation (de-identified for AI); L3 HITL mandatory
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in electoral administration software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian electoral system — INEC (Independent National Electoral Commission) polling unit delineation, ward registers, accreditation data, result declaration; INEC Bimodal Voter Accreditation System (BVAS) awareness; **L3 HITL for all AI** — electoral data is the most politically sensitive data type on the platform
- Registered voter counts (integer), result tallies (integer votes — no floats), accreditation counts, INEC form reference management
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine
- **CRITICAL: Voter PII (names, BVN) must NEVER be stored or processed — only aggregate counts**

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents + ADL political AI cap + L3 HITL rule. Voter PII must never be stored.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: INEC polling unit structure — 176,000+ units, ward-to-unit mapping, BVAS accreditation process, result form (EC8A) structure, constituency collation; key features: polling unit register (unit_code TEXT, ward_name, lga, state, registered_voters INTEGER), election event log (election_name, election_date, accredited_count INTEGER, votes_cast INTEGER), result form reference (form_ref TEXT — no raw results stored; aggregate only), INEC verification tracking.

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give INEC-accredited electoral actors digital polling unit registers, accreditation logs, and aggregate result references — with L3 HITL for all AI
- **Schema:**
  - `polling_unit_profiles` — org_name (e.g., LGA INEC office, CSO observer), INEC_accreditation, state, lga, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `polling_units` — unit_code TEXT, ward_name, lga, state, registered_voters INTEGER, tenant_id
  - `election_events` — election_name, election_date INTEGER, accredited_count INTEGER, votes_cast INTEGER, unit_id, form_ref TEXT, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `inec_accredited` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `CAMPAIGN_INSIGHT` adapted as `ELECTORAL_PARTICIPATION_REPORT` (aggregate accreditation rate, turnout by LGA) — **L3 HITL MANDATORY** — aggregate rates and counts only; no individual voter data; no candidate result breakdown to AI (P13 + electoral data sensitivity); NDPR; USSD excluded (P12)
- **Regulatory hook:** INEC accreditation letter reference at `inec_accredited`
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for data entry operations; Tier 3 for official INEC-contracted digital services
- **ABSOLUTE RULE:** No voter names, BVNs, or individual voter data may be stored or processed in any column. Only aggregate counts.

---

**3. Implementation:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-polling-unit`. `registerPollingUnitVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. **L3 HITL mandatory on ALL AI calls.** Registered voters and vote counts as integers (no floats). No voter PII in any D1 column. No individual result breakdown to AI. Tenant scoped (T3). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-polling-unit/src/polling-unit.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `inec_accredited`; polling unit with integer registered_voters; election event with integer accredited_count, votes_cast; AI participation report with HITL + aiRights + NDPR (aggregate LGA counts only). Negative: voter PII attempt → schema error; candidate result breakdown in AI → P13 blocked; HITL flag missing → blocked; fractional vote count → rejected (integer enforcement); cross-tenant → 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error; Tier 2/3 KYC → 403 for insufficient tier.

**CRITICAL TESTS:**
1. No voter PII can be stored — schema validation rejects any such attempt
2. L3 HITL flag required on ALL AI calls — missing flag = blocking failure
3. Vote counts must be integers — float input rejected

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/polling-unit): Polling Unit vertical — electoral registers, accreditation logs, L3 HITL AI (M12)"
git push origin feat/vertical-polling-unit
```
PR checklist:
- [ ] L3 HITL mandatory on ALL AI routes — blocking if missing
- [ ] No voter PII schema columns — verified
- [ ] Vote counts and accreditation counts as INTEGER — float input rejected
- [ ] `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`
- [ ] PR labeled: `3in1:pillar-1`, `3in1:pillar-3`
- [ ] INEC accreditation gate tested at `inec_accredited` state
- [ ] USSD exclusion enforced for AI (P12); NDPR consent mandatory (P10)
- [ ] T3 on all D1 queries
- [ ] Tests ≥ 15, all passing including L3 HITL enforcement and voter PII schema test
- [ ] Zero typecheck errors (`pnpm -r typecheck`)
- [ ] Zero lint errors (`pnpm -r lint`)
